# 2025 review, 2026 preview
## RIN #10
### Critical minerals
#### Figure 1: Rare earth supply
##### Source: Wood Mackenzie
“The real wild card, on a positive side, in the uranium basis is the changing structure of the uranium market. We have gone to a market where contract arrangements can exist between sellers and buyers that specify the price and terms under which a transaction will take place. That means price stability for producers and consumers, transparency in terms of free cash flow, and junior companies will be able to use off-take agreements and purchase agreements from investment-grade counterparties as additional collateral to finance projects into construction.”

results.title: Rick Rule's 2026 Outlook on Natural Resource Stocks - Where He Sees the Biggest Opportunities | Canadian Mining Report
excerpts:
# Rick Rule's 2026 Outlook on Natural Resource Stocks - Where He Sees the Biggest Opportunities
## Legendary natural resource investor Rick Rule remains highly constructive on the commodity supercycle in 2026, highlighting uranium, copper, selective gold and silver equities, and energy as the areas with the strongest multi-year potential for Canadian and global investors.
Additional context from a March 27, 2026 Kitco interview reinforces this: uranium benefits from the global push for energy security and the recognition that nuclear power is essential for baseload generation in an AI-driven world.
Canadian advantage: Rule repeatedly praises the Athabasca Basin in Saskatchewan as one of the world’s premier uranium districts with low geopolitical risk.
**V. Copper and Oil & Gas – The Next Major Moves**
Copper is Rule’s “next major bull play.” He cites chronic supply deficits and surging demand from electrification, AI data centers, and grid build-out.
From the February 14, 2026 Triangle Investor interview:
